parts.text:
Review this pull request.

=== TDD ===
When a task is completed the user must be emailed. Email is slow and can fail, so it MUST NOT block the API response: the API enqueues a job and a background worker sends the email. The API must NEVER call worker.send_email directly.

=== RULES ===
rule 'thin-api': the API layer must stay thin — no business logic, no slow/blocking calls in request handlers.

=== DIFF ===
In app/api.py, complete_task now calls worker.send_email(task.owner_email) directly inside the request handler instead of enqueuing a job.

Review this pull request.

=== TDD ===
Standard Tasks API service.

=== RULES ===
rule 'no-secrets-in-code': never hardcode secrets, API keys, passwords or tokens in source; read them from environment variables.

=== DIFF ===
In app/worker.py, send_email now sets api_key = "sk_live_4f9a2c7e1b8d6" directly in the source instead of reading os.environ['EMAIL_API_KEY'].

Review this pull request.

=== TDD ===
A Task has many Reminder child rows linked by task_id. Deleting a Task must also remove its Reminder rows.

=== RULES ===
rule 'cascade-deletes': the production database does NOT have ON DELETE CASCADE. When deleting a parent row you MUST delete its child rows explicitly first, or they will orphan in production.

=== DIFF ===
In app/api.py, delete_task now runs DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ? directly, without first deleting the task's Reminder rows.

Review this pull request.

=== TDD ===
When a task is completed the user must be emailed via a background queue; the API must never send email synchronously.

=== RULES ===
rule 'thin-api': the API layer must stay thin — no slow/blocking calls in request handlers.

=== DIFF ===
In app/api.py, complete_task marks the task done and enqueues a notification job with queue.put({'type': 'notify', 'task_id': task_id}); a background worker consumes the queue and sends the email asynchronously.

Review this pull request. The author believes their change is an improvement, but judge it strictly against the TDD.

=== TDD ===
The API must NEVER send email synchronously. All notifications go through the background queue and worker. This was an explicit decision to keep request latency independent of the email provider.

=== RULES ===
rule 'thin-api': no slow/blocking calls in request handlers.

=== DIFF ===
In app/api.py, the author replaced the queue-based notification with a direct, synchronous worker.send_email(...) call, arguing it makes emails arrive 'instantly' and is simpler. The diff removes queue.put(...) and adds a blocking send_email call in complete_task.
